Compliance Centre

Three ISO certifications.
One JAS-ANZ accredited body.
Independently verified.

All certifications are issued by SAI Global under JAS-ANZ accreditation — government-recognised independent verification, not self-declaration.

ISO Certifications Held

CPC holds three current ISO certifications across quality, environmental and occupational health and safety management. All certifications are issued by SAI Global, a JAS-ANZ accredited certification body, and are subject to annual surveillance audits and three-year re-certification cycles.

ISO 9001:2015

Quality Management System

Issued by: SAI Global (JAS-ANZ accredited)

Commercial cleaning services across all CPC operational sites

ISO 14001:2015

Environmental Management System

Issued by: SAI Global (JAS-ANZ accredited)

Environmental management across CPC cleaning operations nationally

ISO 45001:2018

Occupational Health & Safety Management

Issued by: SAI Global (JAS-ANZ accredited)

WHS management for all CPC staff and contractor operations

What JAS-ANZ Accreditation Means

JAS-ANZ — the Joint Accreditation System of Australia and New Zealand — is the government-recognised accreditation authority for conformity assessment bodies operating in Australia and New Zealand. When a certification body such as SAI Global operates under JAS-ANZ accreditation, it means their certification processes have been independently evaluated against internationally recognised standards and found to be competent and impartial.

For procurement teams, this distinction is material. ISO certifications issued by JAS-ANZ accredited bodies are independently verified — the certification body itself is subject to ongoing oversight and re-evaluation. Certifications issued by non-accredited bodies carry no equivalent assurance; they may reflect a self-assessment process rather than genuine independent audit.

All three of CPC's ISO certifications are issued by SAI Global under JAS-ANZ accreditation. Full details of JAS-ANZ and accredited certification bodies are available at jas-anz.org .

Audit Cycle and Currency

Each ISO certification follows a structured audit cycle comprising an annual surveillance audit and a full re-certification audit every three years. Surveillance audits verify that the management system remains operational and effective; re-certification audits involve a comprehensive re-evaluation of the entire system against the relevant standard.

CPC's certifications are current and have been maintained without lapse. Copies of current certificates — including the certification number, scope, issue date and expiry date — are available to procurement teams on request. Certificates can be provided within one business day of a request, along with the name and accreditation number of the issuing body for independent verification.

For tender submissions requiring certificate copies, contact compliance@cpclean.com.au with your tender reference and the specific documents required.

Additional Compliance Documentation

Beyond ISO certifications, CPC maintains a full suite of compliance documentation available to procurement teams. All documents listed below are available on request unless otherwise noted.

Type Document Availability
Statement

Modern Slavery Statement

Annual report under the Modern Slavery Act 2018 (Cth) covering supply chain mapping, risk assessment and remediation actions.

 Published annually
Policy

WHS Management Plan

Documented workplace health and safety management system aligned to ISO 45001 requirements.

 Available on request
Policy

Environmental Management Plan

Operational environmental management procedures aligned to ISO 14001 requirements.

 Available on request
Policy

Quality Management Manual

Documented quality management procedures and processes aligned to ISO 9001 requirements.

 Available on request
Certificate

Public Liability Insurance Certificate

$20 million public liability coverage. Certificate of currency provided with each contract.

 Available on request
Certificate

Professional Indemnity Insurance

Professional indemnity coverage. Certificate of currency available for tender submissions.

 Available on request
Certificate

Workers Compensation: All States

Workers compensation coverage across all states and territories in which CPC operates.

 Available on request

Carbon Neutral Certified Certification

CPC holds Carbon Neutral Certified certification under the Australian Government's carbon neutral program — the national standard for credible carbon neutral claims by Australian businesses. The certification covers Scope 1 (direct emissions), Scope 2 (purchased electricity) and Scope 3 (value chain emissions) as applicable to CPC's cleaning operations.

The carbon neutral claim is independently verified by a carbon neutral approved auditor against the Carbon Neutral Certified Standard for Organisations. Verification and renewal occurs annually, with the certification registered on the carbon neutral website and available for public review.

For contracts where carbon neutral cleaning is a specified requirement or a tender evaluation criterion, CPC can provide the carbon neutral certification record, the verified emissions inventory, and the offset documentation that supports the carbon neutral claim.

Tender Submissions

Need certificates for a tender submission?

Email compliance@cpclean.com.au with your tender reference and the required documents. We respond within 1 business day with current, correctly formatted certificates and documentation ready for submission.
